title: Alcea cretica
intro: Alcea cretica, also known as Syrian hollyhock or Alcea, is a flowering plant species within the mallow family (Malvaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Alcea cretica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and mucilages, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In herbal medicine, Alcea cretica is commonly prepared as a decoction or a syrup, and is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Alcea cretica is classified as a member of the Malvaceae family, which includes hibiscus and okra plants. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other plants in the family. Alcea cretica is further classified as a species within the Alcea genus, which includes several other species of hollyhocks. The Malvaceae family is part of the larger order Malvales, which is a group of flowering plants.
